heading: Maritime Transportation System National Advisory Committee
label: 50402 Maritime Transportation System National Advisory Committee
notes: Amendments2021— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 117–81section 55502 of this title

C, title XXXV, § 3512(b)135 Stat. 2239Jan. 1, 2021Dec. 27, 2021“Notwithstanding any other provision of law—“(1)an advisory committee substantially similar to the Committee established by , United States Code, and that was in force or in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this Act [], including the charter, membership, and other aspects of such advisory committee, may remain in force or in effect for the 2-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this section; andsection 50402 of title 46Jan. 1, 2021“(2)during such 2-year period—“(A)requirements relating the Maritime Transportation System National Advisory Committee established by such section shall be treated as satisfied by such substantially similar advisory committee; and“(B)the enactment of this section shall not be the basis—“(i)to deem, find, or declare such committee, including the charter, membership, and other aspects thereof, void, not in force, or not in effect;“(ii)to suspend the activities of such committee; or“(iii)to bar the members of such committee from a meeting.”

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: There is established a Maritime Transportation System National Advisory Committee (in this section referred to as the “Committee”).

children.heading: .—Function
children.label: b .—Function
children.text: The Committee shall advise the Secretary of Transportation on matters relating to the United States maritime transportation system and its seamless integration with other segments of the transportation system, including the viability of the United States Merchant Marine.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Committee shall consist of 27 members appointed by the Secretary of Transportation in accordance with this section and section 15109.

children.heading: .—Expertise
children.label: 2 .—Expertise
children.text: Each member of the Committee shall have particular expertise, knowledge, and experience in matters relating to the function of the Committee.

children.label: A
children.text: At least one member shall represent the Environmental Protection Agency.

children.label: B
children.text: At least one member shall represent the Department of Commerce.

children.label: C
children.text: At least one member shall represent the Corps of Engineers.

children.label: D
children.text: At least one member shall represent the Coast Guard.

children.label: E
children.text: At least one member shall represent Customs and Border Protection.

children.label: F
children.text: At least one member shall represent State and local governmental entities.

children.label: G
children.text: Additional members shall represent private sector entities that reflect a cross-section of maritime industries, including port and water stakeholders, academia, and labor.

children.label: H
children.text: The Secretary may appoint additional representatives from other Federal agencies as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: comprise more than one-third of the total membership of the Committee or of any subcommittee therein; or

children.label: B
children.text: serve as the chair or co-chair of the Committee or of any subcommittee therein.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee shall be treated as a committee established under chapter 151; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of Transportation shall fulfill all duties and responsibilities and have all authorities of the Secretary of Homeland Security with regard to the Committee.
